quick_facts.label: Credential and provider
quick_facts.value: The National Institute of Open Schooling (NIOS) currently lists the Certificate in Early Childhood Care and Education under its vocational “Teachers Training” registration area as a one-year certificate course.

quick_facts.label: Entry qualification
quick_facts.value: NIOS’s 2025 Vocational Education Prospectus specifies Class 10 pass as the entry level for the ECCE certificate.

quick_facts.label: Medium
quick_facts.value: The NIOS prospectus lists Hindi, English and Urdu as the course media; NIOS regional-centre study-material pages list the ECCE package under course codes 439, 440 and 441 in those languages.

quick_facts.label: Assessment package
quick_facts.value: The certificate is a three-subject vocational package: Understanding the Child (439), Early Childhood Care and Education: Principles and Processes (440), and Organising and Managing an Early Childhood Care and Education Centre (441).

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: For the NIOS Certificate in Early Childhood Care and Education, the stated entry level is a pass in Class 10/Secondary School. The credential is therefore an entry-level vocational option rather than a degree-level teacher-education qualification. Applicants should still verify current document requirements and local study-centre availability in the live NIOS vocational-admission portal before applying.
bullets: Course media listed by NIOS: English, Hindi and Urdu.
The active NIOS portal presents the programme in its Teachers Training category.

sections.heading: Duration and study format
paragraphs: NIOS currently labels this as a “One Year Certificate Course.” The 2025 prospectus also presents the ECCE programme as a one-year certificate and assigns theory and practical/contact components across its three papers. Actual counselling, practical scheduling and centre-level arrangements depend on the Accredited Vocational Institution (AVI) offering the course.
bullets: Programme format: three vocational subjects with theory and practical components.
Study-centre availability should be confirmed through the NIOS vocational portal.

sections.heading: Curriculum and practical emphasis
paragraphs: The NIOS curriculum is organised around understanding young children; ECCE principles and processes; and organising and managing an ECCE centre. Together, these areas connect child development with early-learning practice and with the operational side of a preschool, nursery, crèche or similar setting. NIOS’s published ECCE learning resources also show broader themes relevant to practice, including growth and development, care in early years, play and early learning, developmentally appropriate curriculum planning, inclusion, parent/community involvement, and early identification and intervention.
bullets: 440 — Early Childhood Care and Education: Principles and Processes.
441 — Organising and Managing an Early Childhood Care and Education Centre.
NIOS resource topics include play-based early learning, care, curriculum planning, inclusion and family/community participation.

sections.heading: Admission
paragraphs: NIOS’s live vocational portal shows the programme within the Teachers Training category. Its registration journey is structured around basic details, subject selection, document upload and review. Because admissions, designated projects, participating AVIs/study centres and dates can change by session and location, use the live portal and the study-centre locator rather than relying on an old prospectus or third-party listing.
bullets: Select the ECCE certificate from the NIOS Teachers Training course list when it is available for the relevant admission route.
Complete the portal stages for personal details, subjects, document upload and application review.
Confirm that an appropriate vocational study centre/AVI is available before paying or finalising an application.

sections.heading: Possible next education options
paragraphs: For learners who subsequently have 10+2 or equivalent, IGNOU offers a Diploma in Early Childhood Care and Education (DECE). IGNOU describes DECE as a one-year minimum-duration programme for learners working with children from birth to six years and for people interested in organising or managing child-care settings. This is a separate programme with its own admission rules; the NIOS certificate is not stated by IGNOU as a substitute for the 10+2 eligibility requirement.
bullets: IGNOU DECE minimum duration: 12 months; maximum duration: 24 months.
DECE covers ECCE work in settings such as crèches, preschools, pre-primary classes, nursery schools, anganwadis and balwadis.

useful_links.label: IGNOU Diploma in Early Childhood Care and Education
